title: Man-in-the-Middle Attack (MITM)
description: Intercepting communication between two parties to eavesdrop or manipulate data.
fullExplanation: In a MITM attack, the attacker positions themselves between the victim and the destination, intercepting all traffic. The attacker can read, modify, or inject data. Common techniques include ARP spoofing (Layer 2), DNS spoofing (Layer 7), rogue WiFi access points, and SSL stripping. The victims believe they're communicating directly with each other, but everything flows through the attacker.

techniques.name: ARP Spoofing
techniques.description: Poison ARP caches to redirect traffic through attacker

techniques.name: DNS Spoofing
techniques.description: Return fake DNS responses to redirect victims to attacker's server

techniques.name: Rogue Access Point
techniques.description: Create fake WiFi hotspot with legitimate-sounding name

techniques.name: SSL Stripping
techniques.description: Downgrade HTTPS to HTTP, intercept plaintext

techniques.name: BGP Hijacking
techniques.description: Announce false BGP routes to redirect internet traffic

attackSteps.description: Use ARP spoofing, rogue AP, or DNS poisoning
All packets flow through attacker's machine
Enable IP forwarding so victims don't notice
Read credentials, inject malicious code, steal session tokens
Keep sending spoofed packets to maintain position

keyFacts: MITM is a category of attacks, not a single technique
Requires attacker to be on network path (same subnet or ISP-level)
TLS/SSL prevents reading encrypted traffic (but not metadata)
Certificate pinning prevents fake SSL certificates
HSTS forces HTTPS and prevents SSL stripping
Public WiFi is high-risk for MITM attacks

defenses.name: HSTS
defenses.description: HTTP Strict Transport Security forces HTTPS, prevents downgrade

defenses.name: Certificate Pinning
defenses.description: App only trusts specific certificates, rejects fake ones

defenses.name: VPN
defenses.description: Encrypt all traffic through VPN tunnel

defenses.name: Mutual TLS
defenses.description: Both client and server authenticate with certificates

defenses.name: Avoid Public WiFi
defenses.description: Or use VPN when on untrusted networks
